pi_items.verified_value: Official InVesalius CT Cranium sample dataset from GitHub release v3.0 (0051.zip)

pi_items.source: Downloaded and verified: 24.3 MB zip file containing 108 DICOM files
Extracted and counted DICOM files in downloaded dataset
Analyzed DICOM metadata (Rows and Columns fields)

Cannot verify without running InVesalius mesh generation. Dataset contains 108 slices suitable for generating complex cranial geometry; threshold of 10000 triangles is plausible for bone segmentation.
Cannot verify without running InVesalius mesh generation. Scout series contains 3 slices (36x less data than full series); threshold of 5000 is plausible for limited scout geometry.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the official InVesalius CT Cranium sample dataset (0051.zip) from GitHub release v3.0, available at https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip. The dataset is real (non-synthetic) and contains 108 DICOM CT images of a cranium with dimensions 512×512 pixels, pixel spacing of 0.4785×0.4785 mm, and slice thickness of 2.0 mm. All slices belong to a single homogeneous series. The output STL file is specified as /home/ga/Documents/axial_skull.stl. The triangle count thresholds (min 10,000 for full volume, max 5,000 for scout) cannot be independently verified without executing InVesalius, but are plausible given the 36× difference in data volume between the 3-slice scout and 108-slice full series.
